mission.title: helm: Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is keep
mission.description: Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is keep. Requested by 64+ users.

steps.title: Check current helm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your helm version and configuration:
```bash
helm list --all-namespaces 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working helm installation.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
helm version
```
I wanted to know if there is possibility for Helm install command to ignore existing resource (if already created) if the **helm/resource-policy** is **keep**.

**Scenario:**
1. I created release with Secret containing random generated password.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is…
steps.description:
Alternative implementation to https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7627, https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7625, and https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7575.

Help with validation would be appreciated, but in theory this closes https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6850, closes
```yaml
KIND=deployment
NAME=my-app-staging
RELEASE=staging
NAMESPACE=default
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE
kubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
helm version
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Alternative implementation to https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7627, https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7625, and https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7575.
codeSnippets:
KIND=deployment
NAME=my-app-staging
RELEASE=staging
NAMESPACE=default
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE
kubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE
kubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
